Title: User Registration and Login Flow
Preconditions: User has valid email and password
Steps: 1. Navigate to /register page | 2. Fill in full name, email, phone, and password | 3. Click Register button | 4. Verify redirect to menu page | 5. Logout from the system | 6. Navigate to /login page | 7. Enter registered email and password | 8. Click Login button
Test Data: Full Name: John Doe | Email: john@example.com | Phone: 555-0100 | Password: SecurePass123
Expected Result: User successfully registers with account created in database. JWT token is issued and stored. User is redirected to menu page. After logout and re-login with correct credentials user can access the system.

Title: Browse Menu Items with Categories
Preconditions: User is on the menu page, venue has active menu items and categories
Steps: 1. Navigate to /menu page | 2. Verify all categories are displayed in navigation | 3. Click on a specific category | 4. Verify only items from selected category are shown | 5. Click "All" button | 6. Verify all menu items are displayed | 7. Use search bar to search for an item by name | 8. Verify search results show matching items
Test Data: Category: Appetizers | Search Term: Pizza
Expected Result: All categories load correctly. Clicking a category filters items. Search functionality returns relevant results. Menu items display name

Title: Add Items to Cart and Checkout
Preconditions: User is logged in, cart is initially empty, venue has available menu items
Steps: 1. Navigate to menu page | 2. Click "Add to Cart" on a menu item | 3. Verify success message appears | 4. Click cart icon in navigation | 5. Verify item appears in cart with correct details | 6. Update quantity using +/- buttons | 7. Click "Proceed to Checkout" | 8. Select delivery mode | 9. Fill in delivery address details | 10. Click "Place Order"
Test Data: Item: Margherita Pizza ($12.99) | Quantity: 2 | Address: 123 Main St, New York, 10001 | Phone: 555-0100
Expected Result: Item is added to cart. Cart badge shows correct count. Cart displays item details, price, and quantity controls. Order is created successfully with correct totals (subtotal, tax, delivery fee). Order status is "received".

Title: Real-time Order Tracking with Status Updates
Preconditions: User has placed an order, WebSocket connection is active
Steps: 1. Complete order placement | 2. Navigate to /orders page | 3. Click "Track Order" on recent order | 4. Verify current order status is displayed | 5. Admin/Staff updates order status to "preparing" | 6. Verify status updates in real-time without page refresh | 7. Continue tracking through statuses: ready, out_for_delivery, completed
Test Data: Order ID: Generated during test
Expected Result: Order tracking page displays current status. Status badge updates automatically when admin changes status. WebSocket connection maintains real-time updates. All status transitions (received → preparing → ready → out_for_delivery → completed) are reflected immediately.

Title: Admin Dashboard Metrics and Menu Management
Preconditions: User is logged in as restaurant_admin role
Steps: 1. Login as admin user | 2. Navigate to /admin | 3. Verify dashboard displays daily orders, daily revenue, active menu items, pending orders | 4. Click "Manage Menu" | 5. Navigate to menu management page | 6. Create a new menu item with all details | 7. Edit an existing menu item | 8. Disable a menu item | 9. Verify changes are reflected on customer menu page
Test Data: New Item: Chicken Wings | Price: $8.99 | Category: Appetizers | Image URL: test.jpg
Expected Result: Admin dashboard loads with correct metrics. Menu management allows CRUD operations. New items appear on customer menu. Edited items show updated information. Disabled items are not visible to customers.

Title: Complete Order Journey from Registration to Invoice
Preconditions: System is running, database is clean
Steps: 1. Register new user account | 2. Browse menu and select multiple items | 3. Customize items with add-ons and special instructions | 4. Add items to cart | 5. Review cart and update quantities | 6. Proceed to checkout | 7. Enter delivery details | 8. Place order | 9. Navigate to orders page | 10. Verify order appears with correct details | 11. Admin updates order status to completed | 12. Customer downloads invoice PDF
Test Data: User: testuser@test.com | Items: 2x Pizza ($25.98), 1x Salad ($7.99) | Add-ons: Extra Cheese ($2) | Delivery: 456 Oak Ave, Boston, 02101
Expected Result: End-to-end flow completes without errors. All data persists correctly across pages. Order total calculation is accurate (subtotal $35.97, tax $3.60, delivery $5.00, total $44.57). Invoice PDF generates with correct order details, items, and totals. Payment integration records transaction.

Title: Reorder from Order History
Preconditions: User has at least one completed order in history
Steps: 1. Login as existing customer | 2. Navigate to /orders | 3. Find a completed order | 4. Click "Reorder" button | 5. Verify redirect to cart page | 6. Verify cart contains all items from previous order | 7. Modify quantities if desired | 8. Complete checkout with new delivery address
Test Data: Previous Order: 3x Burger ($29.97), 1x Fries ($4.99) | New Address: 789 Elm St, Chicago, 60601
Expected Result: Reorder button copies all items from previous order to current cart. Item details, customizations, and quantities match original order. Cart total reflects previous order items. Checkout process completes successfully with new delivery address.

Title: Multi-Venue Support and Restaurant Profile Management
Preconditions: Admin has created multiple venues (restaurant, cafe, cloud kitchen)
Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Create 3 venues with different types | 3. Create restaurant profile for each venue with operating hours, service areas | 4. Add menu items specific to each venue | 5. Logout and login as customer | 6. Browse venue 1 menu | 7. Add items to cart | 8. Switch to venue 2 | 9. Verify cart is cleared (different venue) | 10. Complete order for venue 2
Test Data: Venue 1: Italian Restaurant | Venue 2: Coffee Shop | Venue 3: Cloud Kitchen | Each with 5+ menu items
Expected Result: Multiple venues are created successfully. Each venue has independent menu and profile. Customer can browse different venue menus. Cart is venue-specific. Orders are associated with correct venue. Admin can manage each venue independently.

Title: Empty Cart Checkout Attempt
Preconditions: User is logged in, cart is empty
Steps: 1. Navigate directly to /cart URL | 2. Verify empty cart message is displayed | 3. Attempt to access /checkout URL directly
Test Data: No items in cart
Expected Result: Cart page displays "Your cart is empty" message with link to browse menu. Direct access to checkout URL either redirects to cart or menu page. No order can be created without items.

Title: Add Unavailable Item to Cart
Preconditions: Menu item is marked as is_available=false
Steps: 1. Admin sets a menu item to unavailable | 2. Customer navigates to menu page | 3. Locate the unavailable item | 4. Verify "Add to Cart" button is disabled | 5. Attempt to add item via API call directly
Test Data: Item: Unavailable Special ($15.99) | is_available: false
Expected Result: Unavailable items display "Unavailable" status. Add to Cart button is disabled. Direct API calls to add unavailable items return error. Item cannot be added to cart through any method.

Title: Concurrent Cart Updates from Multiple Sessions
Preconditions: User is logged in from two different browsers/devices simultaneously
Steps: 1. Login from Browser A | 2. Login from Browser B with same credentials | 3. Add item X to cart from Browser A | 4. Add item Y to cart from Browser B | 5. Refresh cart in Browser A | 6. Verify both items appear | 7. Remove item from one browser | 8. Verify removal reflects in other browser
Test Data: Item X: Pizza | Item Y: Pasta | User: concurrent@test.com
Expected Result: Cart state synchronizes across sessions. Updates from one session are reflected when other session refreshes. Last write wins for conflicts. No data loss occurs. Cart consistency is maintained.

Title: Invalid Search and Filter Combinations
Preconditions: User is on menu page with search and filter capabilities
Steps: 1. Enter search term with no matching items | 2. Verify "no results" message | 3. Apply dietary filter (vegan) on empty search results | 4. Combine multiple filters (vegan + gluten-free + price range) | 5. Verify results match all filter criteria | 6. Apply impossible filter combination (min price > max price) | 7. Test special characters in search (SQL injection attempt)
Test Data: Search: XYZ123NotExists | Filters: vegan, gluten_free | Price: $0-$5 | Injection: "'; DROP TABLE menu_items; --"
Expected Result: No results message displays for zero matches. Multiple filters work correctly with AND logic. Invalid filter combinations return empty results gracefully. Special characters and SQL injection attempts are safely handled with no errors or security vulnerabilities.

Title: Order Status Notifications
Preconditions: User has placed an order, notification system is active
Steps: 1. Place a new order | 2. Verify order confirmation notification appears | 3. Navigate to /notifications page | 4. Verify notification list shows confirmation | 5. Admin updates order status | 6. Verify status update notification appears | 7. Click notification to view related order | 8. Mark notification as read
Test Data: Order: Test Order #12345 | Notifications: order_confirmation, order_status_update
Expected Result: Order confirmation notification is created on order placement. Status update notifications are sent when order progresses. Notification list displays all user notifications sorted by date. Unread notifications are highlighted. Marking as read updates status. Clicking notification navigates to related order.

Title: Menu Item Customization with Add-ons and Spice Levels
Preconditions: Menu item has multiple add-ons and spice level options available
Steps: 1. Click menu item with customization options | 2. View item details modal | 3. Select multiple add-ons (Extra Cheese, Bacon) | 4. Select spice level (Hot) | 5. Enter special instructions | 6. Adjust quantity to 2 | 7. Add to cart | 8. Verify cart shows customizations | 9. Proceed to checkout | 10. Verify order includes customization details
Test Data: Item: Custom Burger | Add-ons: Extra Cheese ($1), Bacon ($2) | Spice: Hot | Instructions: "No onions" | Qty: 2
Expected Result: Item details modal displays all customization options. Multiple add-ons can be selected. Spice level selection works correctly. Special instructions field accepts text input. Cart displays selected customizations. Order total includes add-on prices. Checkout and order creation preserve all customization details.

Title: Admin Order Management Panel
Preconditions: Admin/Staff user is logged in, multiple orders exist with different statuses
Steps: 1. Login as staff user | 2. Navigate to order management panel | 3. View list of all orders | 4. Filter orders by status (preparing) | 5. Select an order | 6. Update order status to "ready" | 7. Verify customer receives real-time notification | 8. Mark another order as "out_for_delivery" | 9. View order details and customer information
Test Data: Orders: 5 received, 3 preparing, 2 ready | Filter: preparing status
Expected Result: Order management panel displays all orders with filters. Staff can filter by status. Order details show complete information including items, customer details, delivery address. Status updates are successful. Customers receive real-time notifications via WebSocket. Order list refreshes automatically.

Title: Payment Processing with Stripe Integration
Preconditions: Stripe test API keys are configured, user has items in cart
Steps: 1. Complete cart and checkout steps | 2. Place order to initiate payment | 3. Verify payment intent is created | 4. Simulate successful payment via Stripe | 5. Verify order payment status updates to "completed" | 6. Verify invoice is generated | 7. Simulate failed payment | 8. Verify order payment status shows "failed" | 9. Verify order can be retried
Test Data: Test Card: 4242 4242 4242 4242 | Amount: $50.00 | Order ID: Generated
Expected Result: Payment intent is created with correct amount. Stripe payment flow initiates successfully. Successful payment updates order payment_status to "completed". Invoice is auto-generated on payment success. Failed payment updates status to "failed". Payment webhook processes events correctly. Error handling prevents data inconsistencies.

Title: Invalid Login Credentials
Preconditions: User attempts to login with incorrect credentials
Steps: 1. Navigate to /login | 2. Enter valid email but wrong password | 3. Click Login | 4. Verify error message | 5. Enter non-existent email | 6. Click Login | 7. Verify error message | 8. Leave fields empty and submit | 9. Verify validation errors
Test Data: Email: wrong@test.com | Password: WrongPass | Non-existent: notexist@test.com
Expected Result: Login fails with incorrect credentials. Error message displays "Incorrect email or password". No indication whether email exists (security). Empty field validation prevents submission. Multiple failed attempts don't lock account (or implement rate limiting). No sensitive information leaks in error messages.

Title: Unauthorized Access to Admin Endpoints
Preconditions: Regular customer user attempts to access admin-only features
Steps: 1. Login as customer (not admin) | 2. Attempt to navigate to /admin URL | 3. Verify redirect or access denied | 4. Attempt direct API call to create menu item | 5. Attempt to update order status as customer | 6. Attempt to access dashboard metrics API | 7. Verify all requests return 403 Forbidden
Test Data: User Role: customer | Restricted URLs: /admin, /api/menu-items (POST), /api/admin/dashboard
Expected Result: Admin pages redirect customer users to menu or show access denied. API endpoints return 403 Forbidden for unauthorized roles. RBAC middleware correctly enforces role-based access. Customer cannot create, edit, or delete menu items. Customer cannot update order statuses. No privilege escalation vulnerabilities exist.

Title: Boundary Testing for Quantities and Prices
Preconditions: Testing extreme values in cart and order calculations
Steps: 1. Add item with quantity 0 (should default to 1) | 2. Add item with quantity 999 | 3. Verify total calculation | 4. Test negative quantity input | 5. Test decimal quantity (1.5) | 6. Add item with price $0.00 | 7. Add item with price $999999.99 | 8. Verify cart totals handle extremes correctly
Expected Result: Quantity of 0 defaults to minimum 1. Negative quantities are rejected or converted to positive. Decimal quantities are rounded to integers. Large quantities calculate correctly without overflow. Zero-price items are handled. Very large prices don't cause calculation errors. Totals are accurate with proper decimal precision. No integer overflow or floating-point errors.

Title: SQL Injection and XSS Attempts in Forms
Preconditions: User attempts to inject malicious code in various input fields
Steps: 1. Enter SQL injection in search field: "' OR '1'='1" | 2. Verify search returns safe results | 3. Enter XSS script in special instructions: "<script>alert('XSS')</script>" | 4. Submit order and verify script doesn't execute | 5. Test injection in registration fields | 6. Verify all inputs are sanitized and escaped
Test Data: Search: "' OR '1'='1" | Instructions: "" | Name: ""
Expected Result: SQL injection attempts are safely parameterized by ORM. No unauthorized data access occurs. XSS attempts are escaped and rendered as plain text. Scripts do not execute in browser. Input validation sanitizes dangerous characters. Database queries use parameterized statements. No security vulnerabilities are exploitable.

Title: Cart Manipulation via API Tampering
Preconditions: User attempts to manipulate cart prices or quantities via direct API calls
Steps: 1. Intercept cart API request | 2. Modify item price in request payload | 3. Send modified request | 4. Verify server recalculates from database price | 5. Attempt to add item with negative price | 6. Attempt to set quantity above maximum | 7. Verify all validations are server-side
Test Data: Original Price: $10.99 | Tampered Price: $0.01 | Tampered Qty: 10000
Expected Result: Server-side validation recalculates prices from database. Client-side price modifications are ignored. Negative prices are rejected. Maximum quantity limits are enforced. Order totals are always calculated server-side. No client input directly affects final pricing. Audit logs track anomalous requests.,High
